title: quassia pohliana
intro: Quassia pohliana, also known as Quassia amara, is a plant species within the Quassia genus, commonly found in tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Quassia pohliana are attributed to its bark and root, which contain quassin, a compound with natural insecticidal and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and intestinal worms, while also exhibiting potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Quassia pohliana is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat digestive issues and other health ailments.
